content.assessment: A transparency and evidence infrastructure proposal is inherently procedural rather than distributive, so its equity effects are indirect and contingent on whether the data it generates is actually used to correct disparities. The lens evidence supports the underlying diagnosis that Hungary lacks institutionalized tracking of outcomes relevant to equity (contrasted with F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-02's centralized but opaque decision structure) and that existing weak/contested evidence about which villages retain schools (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-06) underscores exactly the kind of measurement gap this proposal would address. A registry could, if designed with segregation and mobility indicators, make visible the currently under-documented equity costs of closures - but a registry alone changes nothing without a mandate connecting findings to decisions.
opportunities: Could explicitly incorporate segregation, intersectional (Roma/non-Roma), and mobility indicators into its viability registry, directly operationalizing lens criteria that are currently under-measured
Could clarify contested findings, such as whether church-maintainer transfers correlate with reduced accountability for segregation (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-03)
strengths: Could resolve the weak/contested evidence problem noted in this lens (e.g., F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-06 on village socioeconomic conditions, F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-03 on church transfers) by establishing standardized, ongoing measurement

Registry tracking of realized outcomes could surface the true distributional impact of closures on Roma and disadvantaged populations, addressing a documented data gap relevant to F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-08's disparities
threats: Delay in establishing this infrastructure occurs while rural enrollment continues to decline (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-01) and while disadvantaged/Roma populations continue to face acute early leaving disparities (F-live-equity_and_source_mobility-08 [sic]) without any real-time protective mechanism

Risk of selective publication or registry capture could mean equity-relevant data (e.g., segregation concentration in North-East Hungary, F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-05) is deprioritized or obscured
weaknesses: Non-mandating design means it cannot on its own alter which villages lose schools or how segregation risk is addressed, given the pattern of centralized administrative decisions (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-02)
Historical pattern of top-down decisions overriding technical evidence suggests an information-only framework may not translate into equity-protective action
